mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the monthly change in UNRATE shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon when both series decline simultaneously.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS change × HOUST change) yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or SPX forward returns do not differ significantly between joint contraction and joint expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets, a sustained increase in the industrial production index (INDPRO) signals productive capacity expansion that lowers input costs and boosts corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: tight labor market with capacity expansion
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns in high INDPRO growth periods are not statistically different from baseline (p > 0.05), or the signal flips direction during recessionary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A CPI reading that exceeds the 215 index level signals entrenched inflationary pressure, prompting market repricing of higher terminal rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL breaches 215, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects robust economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for monthly PAYEMS changes falls below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher during payroll expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of real GDP growth (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirms a broad-based economic upswing that lifts equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint GDPC1-INDPRO growth signal yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX forward returns do not outperform during simultaneous growth acceleration periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sharp decline in the industrial production index (INDPRO) indicates supply chain bottlenecks or capacity constraints that compress corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO declines sharply during high-rate periods, or the signal shows no predictive power for 5-day returns (p > 0.10).
